verbs.meaning: To move your legs very fast, faster than walking
verbs.example: I run to the playground every day after school because I love the swings.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: When you go fast with your feet, you run. Running is faster than walking.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's run to the tree! Ready, set, go!

verbs.meaning: To move at a normal speed using your feet
verbs.example: I walk to school with my mom every morning. It takes fifteen minutes.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Walk is how you move your feet at a normal speed, not too fast.
verbs.dialog_example: Please walk in the hallway. No running at school.

verbs.meaning: To push your body off the ground using your legs
verbs.example: Can you jump over this small puddle? I know you can do it!
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Jump is when your feet leave the ground and you go up in the air.
verbs.dialog_example: Jump as high as you can! Wow, that was high!

verbs.meaning: To jump on one foot
verbs.example: The bunny rabbit hops through the grass looking for carrots to eat.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Hop is like a small jump, like how rabbits move.
verbs.dialog_example: Hop on one foot like a kangaroo!

verbs.meaning: To move forward with little jumps and steps
verbs.example: I skip down the sidewalk when I am happy because it feels like flying.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Skip is a fun way to move - you step and hop, step and hop.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's skip together! One, two, three - skip!

verbs.meaning: To put food in your mouth, chew it, and swallow it
verbs.example: I eat breakfast at 7 o'clock every morning before I brush my teeth.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: When you put food in your mouth and swallow it, you eat.
verbs.dialog_example: Eat your vegetables. They are good for you!

verbs.meaning: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it
verbs.example: You should drink water when you are thirsty, especially after playing outside.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Drink is when you take a sip of water, milk, juice, or any other liquid.
verbs.dialog_example: Drink your milk so you can have strong bones.

verbs.meaning: To rest your body and mind with your eyes closed
verbs.example: I sleep for 10 hours every night because my body needs energy to grow.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Sleep is when your body rests at night and you have dreams.
verbs.dialog_example: It's time to sleep. Put on your pajamas.

verbs.meaning: To stop sleeping and open your eyes
verbs.example: I wake up when the sun comes through my window and the birds start singing.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Wake up is when you open your eyes in the morning after sleeping.
verbs.dialog_example: Wake up, sleepyhead! It's time for school!

verbs.meaning: To take part in an activity for fun and enjoyment
verbs.example: Let's play hide and seek in the backyard! I'll count to twenty while you hide.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Play is when you have fun doing something you enjoy with toys or friends.
verbs.dialog_example: Can I play too? Yes, come join us!

verbs.meaning: To look at and understand written words in a book or on a screen
verbs.example: I read a story about a friendly dragon every night before bed with my dad.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Read is when you look at words in a book and say them in your head or out loud.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's read this book together. You read one page, I read one page.

verbs.meaning: To put words, letters, or numbers on paper using a pencil or pen
verbs.example: I write letters to my grandma because she lives far away and misses me.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Write is when you use a pencil, pen, or crayon to make words or pictures on paper.
verbs.dialog_example: Write your name at the top of the paper.

verbs.meaning: To make a picture using pencils, crayons, markers, or other tools
verbs.example: I draw rainbow unicorns with sparkly pink manes because they are beautiful.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Draw is when you use crayons, markers, or pencils to make a picture.
verbs.dialog_example: Draw a picture of your family. That's wonderful!

verbs.meaning: To fill in a picture with different colors
verbs.example: I color inside the lines of my coloring book with my new markers.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Color is when you add color to a black and white picture.
verbs.dialog_example: Color the sky blue and the grass green.

verbs.meaning: To make musical sounds with your voice
verbs.example: I sing my favorite song in the shower every morning because the echo sounds good.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Sing is when you use your voice to make music with words and melodies.
verbs.dialog_example: Sing along with me! La la la!

verbs.meaning: To move your body rhythmically to music
verbs.example: We dance at birthday parties when the DJ plays fun, happy music that makes us move.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Dance is when you move your body to music, like swaying or jumping to the beat.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's dance! Move your arms and wiggle your hips!

verbs.meaning: To make a happy sound with your mouth when something is funny
verbs.example: I laugh when my dad tells silly jokes like 'Why did the tomato turn red?'
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Laugh is the happy 'ha ha' sound you make when something is funny.
verbs.dialog_example: That joke made me laugh so hard! Ha ha ha!

verbs.meaning: To have tears come from your eyes because you are sad, hurt, or upset
verbs.example: I cry when I miss my best friend who moved to a different city far away.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Cry is when tears fall from your eyes because you feel sad or hurt.
verbs.dialog_example: Why are you crying? Can I help you feel better?

verbs.meaning: To turn up the corners of your mouth to show happiness
verbs.example: I smile when I see my puppy running to me with his tail wagging happily.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Smile is when your mouth makes a happy shape and you show your teeth a little.
verbs.dialog_example: Give me a big smile! Cheese! Beautiful!

verbs.meaning: To turn down the corners of your mouth to show unhappiness
verbs.example: I frown when my little brother takes my toys without asking me first.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Frown is when your mouth makes a sad or upset shape, like an upside-down smile.
verbs.dialog_example: Don't frown. Tell me what's wrong.

verbs.meaning: To do something nice or useful for someone who needs assistance
verbs.example: I help my little brother tie his shoes because he is still learning how.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Help is when you do something nice to make someone's life easier.
verbs.dialog_example: Can you help me clean my room? Of course!

verbs.meaning: To remove dirt, dust, or mess from something to make it tidy
verbs.example: I clean my room every Saturday morning so it looks nice and organized.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Clean is when you wash, wipe, or pick up things to make them neat and not dirty.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's clean up the toys. Put them in the box.

verbs.meaning: To put pieces together to create something new
verbs.example: I build tall towers with my colorful blocks, and then I knock them down!
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Build is when you put pieces or parts together to make something new.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's build a castle with these Legos!

verbs.meaning: To separate something into pieces, often by accident
verbs.example: I try to be careful because I don't want to break my mom's favorite vase.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Break is when something comes apart into pieces, like when you drop a glass.
verbs.dialog_example: Oh no! Did you break your toy? Be careful next time.

verbs.meaning: To repair something that is broken or not working
verbs.example: My dad can fix anything - he fixed my bike when the chain fell off.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Fix is when you make something work again after it breaks.
verbs.dialog_example: Can you fix my doll's arm? I'll try my best.

verbs.meaning: To send something through the air using your hand and arm
verbs.example: I throw the ball to my friend during catch, and he throws it back to me.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Throw is when you use your hand to send something like a ball through the air.
verbs.dialog_example: Throw the ball as far as you can!

verbs.meaning: To grab something that is moving through the air
verbs.example: I catch the ball with both hands so I don't drop it on the ground.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Catch is when you grab something with your hands before it hits the ground.
verbs.dialog_example: Ready? I'll throw. Try to catch it!

verbs.meaning: To hit or move something with your foot
verbs.example: I kick the soccer ball into the goal and score a point for my team!
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Kick is when you use your foot to hit or move something, like a ball.
verbs.dialog_example: Kick the ball to me! Good job!

verbs.meaning: To cover a surface with color using a brush and paint
verbs.example: I paint pictures with watercolors, mixing blue and yellow to make green.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Paint is when you use a brush and colorful liquid to make art.
verbs.dialog_example: Let's paint a beautiful sunset. Use orange and pink!

verbs.meaning: To separate something into pieces using scissors or a knife
verbs.example: I cut paper into heart shapes to make valentines for my friends.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Cut is when you use scissors to separate paper or other things into pieces.
verbs.dialog_example: Cut along the dotted line. Be careful with the scissors!

verbs.meaning: To stick something onto something else using glue
verbs.example: I paste the cut-out stars onto my paper to make a night sky picture.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Paste is when you use glue to stick one thing onto another.
verbs.dialog_example: Paste the circle onto the square. Press it down gently.
